meta.title: MBS Question Bank — User 4 — Career Explorer
meta.delivery: Behaviour-first: forced-choice personality, interest sims, domain simulations, economic games, AI-scored reflection. New v3.3 tests delivered as SJTs/sims.

bias_controls.telemetry: Per-item response latency, long-string & answer-change logging on every block.
bias_controls.attention_checks: Embedded attention-check + infrequency items; careless responders flagged.
bias_controls.validity_band: Each report carries a confidence band (high / interpret-with-caution).
bias_controls.scoring: Forced-choice scored with Thurstonian-IRT; behavioural/game scores weighted above self-report; methods fused by confidence, not naive mean.
bias_controls.overclaiming: Over-claiming module in knowledge section catches exaggeration.
bias_controls.behaviour_first: Personality/interests/work-readiness delivered as forced-choice/SJT/sim, not Likert.

adaptive_rules.session3_routing: Router fires modules by RIASEC top-3 + ability (min 2 T + 3 K + matched M).

items.stem: Age (number/slider — branches to school/college/workforce flow)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-AGE: Age (w=1.0) | ROUTE: Branching (w=1.0)

items.stem: Gender
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-GEN: Gender (w=1.0)

items.stem: Current education level (determines subject list in Phase 11)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-EDU: Education Level (w=1.0) | ROUTE: Branching (w=1.0)

items.stem: Internships you have done (free text + file upload — AI categorises into domain clusters)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-EXP: Work Experience (w=1.0) | CAR-EXP: Career Exposure (w=0.8)

items.stem: Projects you have done (free text + file upload — clustered with internships)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-EXP (w=1.0) | CAR-EXP (w=0.8)

items.stem: Family annual income range (optional — for context only)
items.constructs_fed: DEMO-SES: Socioeconomic Status (w=1.0)

items.stem: Syllogism: All Blops are Frims. Some Frims are Goos. Therefore:  (a) All Blops are Goos  (b) Some Blops may be Goos  (c) No Blops are Goos  (d) All Goos are Blops  [Correct = b]

items.stem: I tailor how I explain something based on who I'm talking to.

items.stem: I rehearse important conversations before having them.

items.stem: I can name the specific emotion I'm feeling, not just 'good' or 'bad'.

items.stem: I notice the physical sensations of my emotions (tight chest, clenched jaw).

items.stem: I take time to name what I'm feeling before acting on it.
items.constructs_fed: EQ-SR (w=1.0) | META-EMO (w=0.8)

items.stem: I can sit with uncomfortable emotions without numbing or avoiding them.
items.constructs_fed: EQ-DT: Distress Tolerance (w=1.0) | EQ-SR (w=0.6)

items.stem: When making a choice, I keep looking until I find the BEST option, even if it takes long.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-MAX: Maximizer (w=1.0)

items.stem: I usually settle for an option that's 'good enough' rather than the perfect one.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-SAT: Satisficer (w=1.0) | DEC-MAX (w=0.5)

items.stem: I often regret decisions soon after making them.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-REG: Decision Regret (w=1.0) | DEC-MAX (w=0.5)

items.stem: I avoid making decisions when I don't have all the information.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-AV: Decision Avoidance (w=1.0) | HOF-UA (w=0.5)

items.stem: I'm comfortable making decisions with incomplete information.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-UNC: Decision Under Uncertainty (w=1.0)

items.stem: I'd rather take a sure ₹5,000 than a 50% chance at ₹20,000.
items.constructs_fed: DEC-RA: Risk Aversion (w=1.0) | I-BIAS-LOSS: Loss Aversion (w=0.6)

items.stem: In group decisions, I voice disagreement even when most people agree.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-DA: Devil's Advocate (w=1.0) | OB-PS: Psychological Safety (w=0.4)

items.stem: I notice when a group is converging too quickly without exploring alternatives.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-GT: Groupthink Awareness (w=1.0) | I-CRT (w=0.4)

items.stem: In teams, I sometimes do less than my share when others are doing well.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-SL: Social Loafing (w=1.0) | BIG5-C (w=0.4)

items.stem: I'd rather make a decision the group can commit to than the optimal one.
items.constructs_fed: TEAM-CON: Consensus Preference (w=1.0)

items.stem: When I disagree, I push to win the argument.
items.constructs_fed: CONF-CMP: Competing (Thomas-Kilmann) (w=1.0) | BIG5-A (w=0.4)

items.stem: I'd rather get my way than have everyone happy.
items.constructs_fed: CONF-CMP (w=1.0) | DISC-D: Dominance (w=0.5)

items.stem: I avoid conflict whenever I can.
items.constructs_fed: CONF-AV: Avoiding (w=1.0) | BIG5-A (w=0.5)

items.stem: I'd rather change the subject than have a hard conversation.
items.constructs_fed: CONF-AV (w=1.0)

items.stem: I've thought about what gives my life meaning.
items.constructs_fed: EXIST-MN: Meaning-Making (Frankl) (w=1.0) | W-SPI (w=0.5)

items.stem: I sometimes feel my life is fundamentally insignificant.
items.constructs_fed: EXIST-NIH: Nihilism Sensitivity (w=1.0) | W-EMO (w=0.5) | W-FLAG (w=0.4)

items.stem: I've had moments when I questioned everything I believed.
items.constructs_fed: EXIST-CRIS: Existential Crisis (w=1.0) | INTRA-REF (w=0.5)

items.stem: I've thought seriously about my own mortality.
items.constructs_fed: EXIST-MOR: Mortality Awareness (w=1.0) | W-SPI (w=0.4)

items.stem: You are managing investors' money. A risky choice could make you a big bonus but expose them to loss. Best action: (a) Take the risk — bigger reward justifies it; (b) Disclose the trade-off and let investors decide; (c) Avoid risk altogether to protect your bonus structure; (d) Take a smaller related position quietly.
items.constructs_fed: AGENCY: Agency Theory: principal-agent alignment (w=1.0)

items.stem: A regulator informally signals everyone in your industry is moving to Standard X. You would: (a) Adopt X to fit in; (b) Wait and see; (c) Refuse unless required by law; (d) Help shape what X means.
items.constructs_fed: INST: Institutional Theory case (w=1.0) | ISO: Isomorphism: coercive/mimetic/normative (w=1.0)

items.stem: A new policy is profitable but will hurt one community group. You would most likely: (a) Prioritise shareholder return only; (b) Map all stakeholders before deciding; (c) Drop the policy; (d) Run a small pilot and adjust.
items.constructs_fed: STAKE: Stakeholder Theory orientation (w=1.0) | OB-022: Ethical attitude (w=0.4)

items.stem: Identify your company's strongest competitive advantage from these four: (a) A patented technology only you own; (b) Lowest-cost producer in the market; (c) Best customer service in your city; (d) Most charismatic CEO.
items.constructs_fed: RBV: Resource-Based View judgement (w=1.0) | VRIN: VRIN test (Valuable/Rare/Inimitable/Non-substitutable) (w=1.0)

items.stem: Your firm relies on ONE supplier for 70% of inputs. The best strategic move is: (a) Negotiate a tighter contract; (b) Build a second supplier in parallel; (c) Vertically integrate (buy the supplier); (d) Stay dependent — the supplier is reliable.
items.constructs_fed: RDT: Resource Dependence Theory (w=1.0) | ROT: Resource Orchestration (w=0.6)

items.stem: Your start-up has 5 founders, 0 process and 12 customers. The right next move is: (a) Hire a CFO and CHRO immediately; (b) Build minimum formal process for sales and product; (c) Stay informal forever — process kills innovation; (d) Sell the company while it's small.
items.constructs_fed: LIFECYCLE: Organisational Life-Cycle stage diagnosis (w=1.0)

items.stem: Over the past two weeks, I have felt calm, hopeful and emotionally steady.
items.constructs_fed: W-EMO: Emotional wellness (w=1.0) | BIG5-N: Neuroticism (w=0.5)

items.stem: I have at least one person I can talk to honestly when things are hard.
items.constructs_fed: W-SOC: Social wellness (w=1.0) | SDT-REL: Relatedness (w=0.5) | ATT-S: Attachment Secure (w=0.4)

items.stem: I get enough sleep, move my body, and eat in a way that supports my energy.
items.constructs_fed: W-PHY: Physical wellness (w=1.0) | SELF-SC: Self-Control (w=0.5)

items.stem: I am learning new things that genuinely interest me right now.
items.constructs_fed: W-INT: Intellectual wellness (w=1.0) | MIND-G: Growth Mindset (w=0.4) | LR-CUR: Curiosity-driven learning (w=0.6)

items.stem: I feel in control of my money and not anxious about my immediate financial situation.
items.constructs_fed: W-FIN: Financial wellness (w=1.0) | MASLOW-1: Safety needs (w=0.4)

items.stem: I have a clear sense of purpose or values that guides my choices.
items.constructs_fed: W-SPI: Spiritual wellness (w=1.0) | MASLOW-5: Self-Actualisation (w=0.5) | GMI-EX: Existential intelligence (w=0.5)

items.stem: Native language / language of comfort

items.stem: Location (city / state)
